mnemonics: CIA Group: Closure, Identity, Associativity, Inverse
keyPoints: A group is a set combined with an operation that satisfies closure, associativity, identity, and invertibility.
An abelian group is a group whose operation is also commutative.
The order of a group is the number of elements in its set.
Subgroups are subsets of a group that themselves form a group under the same operation.
commonMistakes: Assuming all operations are commutative (e.g., matrix multiplication is not, but integer addition is).
Forgetting to check the identity element is in the set when proving a subset is a subgroup.
Confusing the identity element (e.g., 0 for addition, 1 for multiplication).
